result:
Milestone authored and decomposed. Summary of what I emitted.

## Milestone: `milestone-02-provider-dispatch`

I read the spec (the five-level paradigm + wave-derivation rules) and the live code — `pkg/dispatch` (`Subagent`, `ProviderSpec`, `Usage`), `internal/subagent/anthropic/` layering, `internal/controller/dispatch_helpers.go` (`ResolveProvider`/`resolveImage`), `internal/dispatch/podjob/jobspec.go` (credproxy + `ProviderSecretRef` env wiring), the v1alpha1 `SubagentConfig`/`LevelConfig` schema, and the `verify-dispatch-imports` / `providerfirewall` guards — to anchor the decomposition in the real foundation rather than the rebuilt-from-scratch assumption.

**`children/MILESTONE.md`** — single user-visible outcome: an operator runs one Project with a Claude planner pool + Codex executor pool to completion, with spec wave-boundary failure semantics holding unchanged across providers. Exit criteria pin the load-bearing constraints (provider firewall, `Usage` normalization with the OpenAI cache mapping, vendor-sentinel fail-fast, Secret-ref-only creds, offline tests).

**Five Phase child CRDs** over a shallow DAG (`milestoneRef` wires each to the milestone):

| Phase | dependsOn | Deliverable boundary |
|---|---|---|
| `phase-01-codex-subagent-core` | — | `internal/subagent/codex/` package: client, `Run` over `codex exec`, JSONL parser, pricing table + `Usage` normalization, doc |
| `phase-02-codex-image-binary` | 01 | `cmd/codex-subagent` shim + multi-stage Dockerfile bundling the Codex CLI |
| `phase-03-per-level-provider-switch` | — | `ResolveProvider` derives Vendor per level (kills the hard-coded `"anthropic"`); firewall intact |
| `phase-04-credential-and-chart-wiring` | 03 | `OPENAI_API_KEY`/`CODEX_API_KEY` Secret-ref routing + chart values |
| `phase-05-heterogeneous-dispatch-demo` | 01,02,03,04 | mixed-provider example Project + multi-phase e2e proving cross-provider wave-boundary failure semantics |

**Parallelism the dependency declarations permit:** wave 1 = {P1, P3} (independent roots), wave 2 = {P2, P4}, wave 3 = {P5 capstone}. The two independent roots (Codex package vs. controller-side switch) plan and build concurrently because they share no code edge — the controller never imports the Codex package, by design.

Only the machine contract (`*.json`) and the human-review `MILESTONE.md` were written into `children/`. No Plan or Task CRDs emitted — those belong to the downstream phase/plan planners.
